excluded_defs.name: decode
excluded_defs.reason: read: investigated to understand whether surrogates could reach UTF8_ACCEPT; they cannot with the Bjoern decoder because state 5 (after 0xED) rejects 0xA0..0xBF type bytes

exploration_log.aha_moment: The fix belongs in the UTF8_ACCEPT default case: while the Bjoern decoder prevents real surrogates from reaching this path today, the explicit guard makes the intent clear and defends against future decoder changes.

dead_ends.explored: Investigated how Bjoern UTF-8 decoder handles WTF-8 lone surrogates (0xED 0xA0 0x80)
dead_ends.why_irrelevant: The decoder already rejects those byte sequences at the UTF8_REJECT path; the fix is about the UTF8_ACCEPT path for when a codepoint in the surrogate range is decoded

exploration_log.hindsight: Would have been useful to trace the Bjoern decoder table for all ED-prefixed sequences first before guessing the failure mode.

alternatives: Add check in UTF8_ACCEPT default case for codepoints 0xD800..0xDFFF
Add check in the incomplete multibyte default case
Change decoder to explicitly track surrogates
key_decisions.decision: Add surrogate codepoint check in the UTF8_ACCEPT default case, matching the pattern of the existing replace logic
key_decisions.reasoning: The UTF8_ACCEPT case is where valid-looking codepoints are processed; the replacement pattern from the REJECT path can be reused verbatim

search_sequence.result: Three cases: UTF8_ACCEPT (valid codepoints), UTF8_REJECT (invalid bytes), default (incomplete multibyte). Fix belongs in UTF8_ACCEPT default case at line 460

search_sequence.action: Traced Bjoern decoder table for 0xED sequences
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to verify whether surrogates could actually reach UTF8_ACCEPT
search_sequence.result: State 5 (after 0xED) rejects type-7 bytes (0xA0..0xBF), so WTF-8 surrogates are caught by UTF8_REJECT path; fix in ACCEPT case is defensive

solve_notes: The `dump_escaped` method processes codepoints decoded from the input string via the Bjoern UTF-8 DFA. In the `UTF8_ACCEPT` default case, codepoints that decode successfully are normally passed through or escaped; I added a guard that intercepts codepoints in the surrogate range (0xD800..0xDFFF) when `error_handler == replace` and emits the Unicode replacement character `\ufffd` using the same byte sequences already used in the `UTF8_REJECT` replacement path.
task_text:
Fix serializer emitting invalid UTF-8 replacement on lone surrogates

The serializer in `detail/output/serializer.hpp` replaces invalid
UTF-8 sequences with `\ufffd` when `error_handler_t::replace` is
active, but for lone high surrogates in `\uD800`–`\uDBFF` it emits
raw bytes instead of the replacement character. Fix the
`dump_escaped` method to detect unpaired surrogates and apply the
replacement consistently.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: decode
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows the UTF-8 DFA that drives the switch in dump_escaped; understanding its states (UTF8_ACCEPT/UTF8_REJECT/incomplete) is needed to place the fix correctly

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just dump_escaped because it is the only function edited. The thrash_preventing set adds decode because an agent would need to understand what decode returns in the switch cases (ACCEPT vs REJECT vs incomplete) to know which case to add the surrogate check to.
